name: Research Article Catalog Taxonomy
description: A hierarchical taxonomy for cataloging research articles using a three-level structure: Category → Theme → Subject. Designed to support consistent tagging, browsing, and analytics across a research article repository.

nodes.name: Hierarchy & Rules
nodes.description: Structural conventions and governance rules for applying the taxonomy.

children.name: Hierarchy Levels
children.description: Defines the required levels and their intent.

children.name: Category
children.description: Top-level domain grouping used for broad navigation and reporting.

children.name: Theme
children.description: Mid-level grouping representing a coherent area within a category.

children.name: Subject
children.description: Fine-grained topic used for precise filtering and tagging of articles.

children.name: Assignment Rules
children.description: Guidelines for how articles should be classified.

children.name: Primary Classification
children.description: Each article should have exactly one primary Category and one primary Theme; at least one Subject is required.

children.name: Secondary Tags
children.description: Optional additional Themes/Subjects may be added for cross-cutting topics.

children.name: Naming Conventions
children.description: Use title case for display names; avoid abbreviations unless widely standard.

nodes.name: Taxonomy
nodes.description: The Category → Theme → Subject hierarchy for research article cataloging.
